items.title: 2026/578: Methodist Church, Hurstlands, Hurst Green, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0HG
items.description: Demolition of deconsecrated church building. Redevelopment of former methodist church site to provide a four-storey block of sixteen flats, comprising six one-bedroom flats, eight two-bedroom flats and two three-bedroom flats
items.headline: Plans Submitted for 16 Flats on Hurst Green Church Site
items.summary: A planning application has been lodged to demolish the deconsecrated Methodist Church in Hurstlands and build a four-storey block of 16 flats. The scheme would provide six one-bedroom, eight two-bedroom and two three-bedroom homes.

data.address: Methodist Church, Hurstlands, Hurst Green, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0HG

items.description:
Please refer to photos provided:

T3) - Macrocarpa within neighbouring property overhanging right-hand boundary of front garden - To be cut back from garage and house creating at least 2m clearance.

items.summary: An application has been made to prune two protected trees at 140 Bluehouse Lane, Limpsfield. A yew overhanging the driveway would be crown lifted and cut back, while a neighbouring macrocarpa would be trimmed to give clearance from the house and garage.

items.title: 2026/743/TPO: 8 Farley Park, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 9HY
items.description:
Please refer to photos provided: 
T1) - Red Oak on right-hand side of front garden - To crown reduce to previous points, removing up to 2m growth to a residual height of 14m and crown spread of 11m. Crown lifted to 5m from ground level over road and 2.5m over garden / driveway. Removing limbs <10cm dia. Repeat pruning for good arboricultural management.
items.headline: Tree work proposed for protected red oak in Farley Park
items.summary: A planning application asks to prune a protected red oak in the front garden of 8 Farley Park, Oxted. The work would reduce the crown by up to 2m and lift branches over the road and driveway as part of routine tree care.
